_fixture_note: SANITIZED backward-compat fixture. A structurally faithful 1.2.0-shaped analysis (no element_counts) used to prove such a document still validates under ANALYSIS_SCHEMA 1.3.0. All confidential part numbers, drawing numbers, firmware paths, absolute source paths, IPCAT document IDs and internal SoC/donor codenames have been replaced with generic placeholders. Shape and field types are preserved, including the fully-populated SoundWire path (amplifiers/mics/speakers non-empty, soundwire.present=true, master_count=1), the cross-lane ambiguity example (master_count could be 1 or 2), the schematic_nets array and a target-specific ipcat_findings result. This models a SoundWire-attached codec + smart-amp + DMIC/AMIC target.

board.value: Target-C EVK: SOC CARD + carrier board + Audio Daughtercard (CODEC-B, 2x AMP-B) + MIC Daughtercard (8x DMIC, 2x AMIC)

codecs.role: primary audio codec — SoundWire slave (SWR_RX/SWR_TX to LPASS via level-shifted B2B connector); provides AMIC1-3, HPH L/R, AUX_OUT, HS_DET/HPH_REF, MIC_BIAS
citations: targets/target-c/evidence/offline/board-block-diagram.pdf p3 (BLOCK DIAGRAM, U labeled 'CODEC-B' in AUDIO DC block with SWR_RX/SWR_TX, AMIC1-3, HPH, AUX_OUT pins)
targets/target-c/evidence/offline/board-block-diagram.pdf p5 (B2B CONNECTOR sheet: CODEC-B HSDET/HPH/AUX/RST/AMIC/SWR_TX/RX nets)
ipcat doc <ipcat-doc-1>: 'this SoC can be paired with the CODEC-B codec family via SoundWire interface'
kernel driver family present but exact CODEC-B compat string not found: kernel-b/sound/soc/codecs/codec-b.c (of_match table lists sibling variants only — no vendor,codec-b entry)
kernel-b/sound/soc/codecs/codec-b-sdw.c (MODULE_DEVICE_TABLE(sdw, codec_b_slave_id))

amplifiers.role: 2x stereo smart speaker Class-D amplifiers (Speaker 0 / Speaker 1), each a SoundWire slave with its own SWR0 CLK/DATA pair, ENABLE from LPASS GPIO
citations: targets/target-c/evidence/offline/board-block-diagram.pdf p3 (BLOCK DIAGRAM: two amp instances explicitly labeled 'AMP-B' driving Speaker 0 / Speaker 1 via SPK0/1_OUTP/N, with SWR0, ENABLE, supply rails)
upstream driver confirms exact part support: kernel-b/sound/soc/codecs/amp-b.c (OTP id table lists AMP-B and siblings) and MODULE_DEVICE_TABLE(sdw, amp_b_swr_id)
ipcat doc <ipcat-doc-1>: 'external speaker amplifiers are connected via a dedicated SoundWire interface'

mics.role: 8 digital mics on MIC DC, muxed via analog muxes to DMIC01/23/45/67 CLK+DATA pairs into CODEC-B/LPASS
citations: targets/target-c/evidence/offline/board-block-diagram.pdf p3 (BLOCK DIAGRAM: 'DMIC X 8' block in MIC DC, DMIC_BIAS from an LDO)
targets/target-c/evidence/offline/board-block-diagram.pdf p4 (B2B CONNECTOR: DMIC01/23/45/67_CLK,DATA nets, DMIC supply rails, DMIC_ELDO_EN)
ipcat doc <ipcat-doc-1>: 'this SoC can support up to 8 DMICs directly connected to the SoC'

mics.role: 2 analog mics on MIC DC feeding CODEC-B AMIC1/AMIC2 analog inputs directly (codec exposes AMIC1-3 but only 2 populated per schematic)
citations: targets/target-c/evidence/offline/board-block-diagram.pdf p3 (BLOCK DIAGRAM: 'AMIC X 2' block in MIC DC, AMIC_BIAS 1,2,3 routed to CODEC-B MIC_BIAS/AMIC1/AMIC2 pins)

speakers.role: left/primary channel, SPK0_OUTP/N to Speaker Header CONN0
citations: targets/target-c/evidence/offline/board-block-diagram.pdf p3

speakers.role: right/secondary channel, SPK1_OUTP/N to Speaker Header CONN1 (stereo pair)
citations: targets/target-c/evidence/offline/board-block-diagram.pdf p3

buses: I2S/PCM/TDM — 5 instances total (4 in LPI: LPI_I2S0/1/2/3, 1 in Core), muxed via analog muxes, level-shifted 1.2V<->1.8V across the SOC-card-to-audio-DC B2B connector (JC1/JC2)
SoundWire — SWR_RX/SWR_TX (2-wire) to CODEC-B codec, plus separate SWR0 per AMP-B amp; controller/master side lives on the SoC card, not yet present as a DT node in the applied soc-b.dtsi
DMIC PDM bus — DMIC01/23/45/67 CLK+DATA pairs (4 stereo pairs = 8 DMICs) into LPASS/codec
SLIMbus — referenced by IPCAT for BT audio, not evidenced in schematics reviewed

citations: targets/target-c/evidence/offline/board-block-diagram.pdf p3 (BLOCK DIAGRAM shows single SWR_RX/SWR_TX pair to CODEC-B plus SWR0 to each AMP-B, all originating from the SOC CARD side, level-shifted through a single shifter block)

No SoundWire controller compatible string (e.g. vendor,soundwire-vX.Y.Z) present yet in kernel-b/arch/arm64/boot/dts/soc-b.dtsi — master_count cannot be confirmed from kernel DT

nearest_targets.name: target-a
nearest_targets.rationale: Shares the generic ADSP/AudioReach plumbing pattern (rpmhpd LCX/LMX power domains feeding remoteproc_adsp, same GPR/Q6APM/Q6PRM driver stack), but the audio codec topology is fundamentally different: target-a uses two discrete I2S-attached ICs (a DAC and an ADC, no SoundWire) while this target uses a SoundWire-attached codec plus 2x SoundWire smart amps with DMIC/AMIC front-end. No SoundWire-based comparable target exists among the supplied candidates.
citations: persistent memory: target-a-audio-facts.md (discrete I2S DAC/ADC pair, SCMI power domains not rpmhpd)
target-c evidence as cited above

missing_evidence: No SoundWire controller DT node (vendor,soundwire-vX.Y.Z) exists yet in the applied soc-b.dtsi/soc-b-mtp.dts — SoundWire master_count and controller register base are unconfirmed from kernel-side evidence.

No LPASS macro nodes (WSA/VA/RX/TX macro), no GPR/Q6APM/Q6PRM DT nodes, and no sound-card (machine driver) node exist anywhere in the applied kernel tree for this target — all audio DT wiring is still pending as unapplied patches, not yet present in the kernel tree at the designated commit.
The board-level audio DTS file referenced by candidate patches does not exist in this kernel tree; only soc-b.dtsi (SoC) and soc-b-mtp.dts (display/USB/UFS-only MTP board) were found via Glob.

Exact CODEC-B compatible string is absent from the upstream codec-b.c of_match table (only sibling variants present) — unclear whether CODEC-B is a drop-in variant of the existing driver or needs a new match entry.

GPIO/pinmux assignments for I2S0-3 (BCLK/WS/DATA), SWR_RX/TX, and DMIC clk/data lines are visible as net names on the schematic (B2B connector, mux, level-shifter sheets) but have not been cross-referenced against the SoC pinctrl driver to obtain actual GPIO numbers.

SoundWire master_count is ambiguous: schematic shows one SWR_RX/TX pair to the codec and a separate 'dedicated' SWR0 to the amps, which IPCAT text also implies are 'dedicated' — could be 1 or 2 physical master instances; not resolved.

AUDIO CODEC and AUDIO SPEAKER 0/1 detail sheets and the amplifier datasheet PDF were not opened in this session — exact SoundWire slave addresses, port mapping, and amp gain/config straps remain unverified.
No mic bias / AMIC electrical net detail beyond the block diagram was reviewed; AMIC1-3 vs only-2-populated-AMIC discrepancy not resolved against a detail sheet.

candidate_targets list contains no real SoundWire-based comparable target — the kernel_history_donor_hint entries are Linux subsystem topic labels tied to commit SHAs, not actual bring-up targets, so 'nearest target' ranking is weak by necessity.
IPCAT SoundWire Master HPG hits returned only generic multi-project boilerplate with no target-specific master instance count or register map.

ipcat_findings.notes: Queried the IPCAT content search for target-specific audio interface / SoundWire info. Got several hits: some from an 'LPASS HPG' document that explicitly names this target and describes its audio interface topology by name (codec family via SoundWire, amp family via dedicated SoundWire, up to 8 DMICs, 5 I2S/PCM/TDM interfaces, BT via SLIMbus) — this is genuinely target-specific and corroborates/extends the schematic findings (part-family confirmation for both the codec and the amp). The remaining hits were from generic 'Soundwire_master Hardware Programming Guide' documents describing generic multi-project SWR master architecture with no target-specific master count or register map — these were generic/boilerplate.
